children.name: Schistochlamys
children.description: Schistochlamys — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Schistochlamys melanopis
children.description: The black-faced tanager is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Schistochlamys ruficapillus
children.description: The cinnamon tanager is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is found in Argentina, Brazil, and Paraguay. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests, dry savanna, subtropical or tropical dry shrubland, and heavily degraded former forest.

children.name: Hemithraupis
children.description: Hemithraupis — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Hemithraupis flavicollis
children.description: The yellow-backed tanager is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae, the tanagers. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, French Guiana, Guyana, Peru, Suriname, and Venezuela; also extreme eastern Panama in Central America. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and heavily degraded former forest.

children.name: Hemithraupis guira
children.description: The guira tanager is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Hemithraupis ruficapilla
children.description: The rufous-headed tanager is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to Brazil.

children.name: Tangara
children.description: Tangara — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Tangara argentea
children.description: Tangara argentea — a species in the genus Tangara, family Thraupidae. Also known as tanagers & allies.

children.name: Tangara cyanocephala
children.description: The red-necked tanager is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Tangara dowii
children.description: The spangle-cheeked tanager is a medium-sized passerine bird. This tanager is an endemic resident breeder in the highlands of Costa Rica and western Panama.

children.name: Tangara fastuosa
children.description: The seven-colo[u]red tanager is a vulnerable species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to forests in north-eastern Brazil. It resembles the overall greener green-headed tanager; a species confusingly known as the 'seven-coloured tanager' (saíra-sete-cores) in Portuguese.

children.name: Tangara labradorides
children.description: The metallic-green tanager is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is found in Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Tangara nigroviridis
children.description: The beryl-spangled tanager is a small songbird of the northern Andes.

children.name: Tangara seledon
children.description: The green-headed tanager is a brightly colored bird found in the Atlantic forest in south-eastern Brazil, far eastern Paraguay, and far north-eastern Argentina.

children.name: Heterospingus
children.description: Heterospingus — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Wetmorethraupis
children.description: Wetmorethraupis — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Dacnis modesta
children.description: Dacnis modesta — a species in the genus Dacnis, family Thraupidae. Also known as tanagers & allies.

children.name: Ramphocelus
children.description: Ramphocelus — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Ramphocelus icteronotus
children.description: The flame-rumped tanager is a passerine bird in the tanager family Thraupidae. It is found from Panama to western Ecuador in semi-open forest and woodland. The subspecies with a yellow rump from the Cauca Valley in Colombia, the lemon-rumped tanager, has sometimes been considered as a separate species.

children.name: Eucometis
children.description: Eucometis — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Eucometis penicillata
children.description: The grey-headed tanager is a widely distributed species of small Neotropical bird in the tanager family Thraupidae. It is the only member of the genus Eucometis.

children.name: Chrysothlypis
children.description: Chrysothlypis — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Chrysothlypis chrysomelas
children.description: The black-and-yellow tanager is a fairly small passerine bird. This tanager resides in the hills of Costa Rica and Panama.

children.name: Chrysothlypis salmoni
children.description: The scarlet-and-white tanager is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is found in Colombia and northern Ecuador. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and heavily degraded former forest.

children.name: Cypsnagra
children.description: Cypsnagra — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Cypsnagra hirundinacea
children.description: The white-rumped tanager is a South American bird in the tanager family Thraupidae. It is the only member of the genus Cypsnagra.

children.name: Tersina
children.description: Tersina — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Tersina caerulea
children.description: Tersina caerulea — a species in the genus Tersina, family Thraupidae. Also known as tanagers & allies.

children.name: Tersina viridis
children.description: The swallow tanager is a species of Neotropic bird in the tanager family Thraupidae. It is the only member of the genus Tersina. It is found widely throughout South America, from eastern Panama to far northern Argentina.

children.name: Neothraupis
children.description: Neothraupis — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Neothraupis fasciata
children.description: The shrike-like tanager, also known as the white-banded tanager, is a South American bird in the tanager family Thraupidae. It is the only member of the genus Neothraupis.

children.name: Tachyphonus
children.description: Tachyphonus — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Tachyphonus surinamus
children.description: The fulvous-crested tanager is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae, the tanagers.

children.name: Charitospiza eucosma
children.description: The coal-crested finch is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. Though previously classified in the bunting and American sparrow family Emberizidae, molecular phylogenetic studies have shown that it is a member of the large tanager family Thraupidae. It is the only member of the genus Charitospiza.

children.name: Coryphospingus
children.description: Coryphospingus — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Coryphospingus cucullatus
children.description: The red pileated finch, also known as the red-crested finch, is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Ecuador, French Guiana, Guyana, Paraguay, and Peru, on the eastern side of the Andes. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests, subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, subtropical or tropical dry shrubland, and heavily degraded former forest.

children.name: Coryphospingus pileatus
children.description: The grey pileated finch, also known as the pileated finch, is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae, where it has recently been moved to from the Emberizidae. It is found in Brazil, Colombia, French Guiana, and Venezuela in subtropical or tropical dry forests, subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, subtropical or tropical dry shrubland, and heavily degraded former forest.

children.name: Melanospiza
children.description: Melanospiza — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Melanospiza bicolor
children.description: The black-faced grassquit is a small bird. It is recognized as a tanager closely related to Darwin's finches. It breeds in the West Indies except Cuba, on Tobago but not Trinidad, and along the northern coasts of Colombia and Venezuela.

children.name: Melanospiza richardsoni
children.description: The Saint Lucia black finch is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to Saint Lucia, where its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest, subtropical or tropical dry shrubland, and plantations.

children.name: Incaspiza
children.description: Incaspiza — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Incaspiza laeta
children.description: The buff-bridled Inca finch is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to Peru where its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests and subtropical or tropical high-altitude shrubland.

children.name: Incaspiza ortizi
children.description: The grey-winged Inca finch is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Incaspiza personata
children.description: The rufous-backed Inca finch is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Incaspiza pulchra
children.description: The great Inca finch is a species of passerine bird in the tanager family Thraupidae. It was traditionally placed in the family Emberizidae. It is endemic to Peru.

children.name: Incaspiza watkinsi
children.description: The little Inca finch is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to Peru where its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical dry shrubland. It is threatened by habitat loss.

children.name: Poecilostreptus palmeri
children.description: The grey-and-gold tanager is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is found in Colombia, Ecuador, and Panama . Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Phonipara
children.description: Phonipara — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Phonipara canora
children.description: The Cuban grassquit is a small bird in the tanager family Thraupidae. It is endemic to Cuba.

children.name: Chalcothraupis
children.description: Chalcothraupis — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Chalcothraupis ruficervix
children.description: The golden-naped tanager is a species of bird in the tanager family Thraupidae. It is found in South America from Colombia to Bolivia. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist montane forests and heavily degraded former forest.

children.name: Rhopospina
children.description: Rhopospina — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Rhopospina fruticeti
children.description: The mourning sierra finch is a species of South American bird in the tanager family Thraupidae.

children.name: Rauenia
children.description: Rauenia — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Rauenia bonariensis
children.description: The blue-and-yellow tanager is a species of bird in the tanager family Thraupidae.

children.name: Heliothraupis
children.description: Heliothraupis — a genus of tanagers & allies in the family Thraupidae.

children.name: Heliothraupis oneilli
children.description: The Inti tanager, also known as the San Pedro tanager, is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is the only member of the genus Heliothraupis. It is restricted to the lower Yungas of western Bolivia and southern Peru.
